# CircuitKit Community Code of Conduct

## Our Commitment

We are committed to providing a welcoming and inspiring community for all. CircuitKit is dedicated to being an inclusive, safe space where all people are valued and respected.

## Our Values

- **Inclusivity**: We welcome contributors of all backgrounds and experiences
- **Respect**: We treat all community members with courtesy and respect
- **Collaboration**: We work together toward common goals
- **Excellence**: We strive for quality in our code and our interactions
- **Safety**: We maintain a harassment-free environment

## Expected Behavior

All community members are expected to:

- Be respectful and constructive in all interactions
- Welcome newcomers and help them get oriented
- Focus on what is best for the community
- Show empathy towards other community members
- Respect differing opinions and experiences
- Accept responsibility for mistakes and learn from them
- Be patient and assume good faith

## Unacceptable Behavior

We do not tolerate:

- Harassment, discrimination, or bullying of any kind
- Offensive comments related to personal characteristics or identities
- Unwelcome sexual attention or advances
- Deliberate intimidation or threats
- Trolling, insults, or derogatory comments
- Publishing private information without consent
- Other conduct that could reasonably be considered inappropriate

## Scope

This Code of Conduct applies to:
- All CircuitKit spaces (GitHub, discussions, issues, email)
- Community events and conferences where CircuitKit is represented
- Official CircuitKit communications

## Enforcement

Instances of abusive, harassing, or unacceptable behavior may be reported by:
- Opening a private GitHub issue
- Contacting us at circuitkit@example.com
- Reaching out to project maintainers

All reports will be reviewed and investigated. We commit to maintaining confidentiality.

## Consequences

Violations of this Code of Conduct may result in:
- A request to clarify intent or behavior
- A temporary or permanent ban from the community
- Other actions as deemed appropriate

## Attribution

This Code of Conduct is adapted from the [Contributor Covenant](https://www.contributor-covenant.org/) (v2.0).

## Questions?

If you have questions about this Code of Conduct, please contact the maintainers at circuitkit@example.com.

---

**Thank you for helping make CircuitKit a welcoming and supportive community!**
